optimizer_switches.name: semijoin
optimizer_switches.explanation: IN / EXISTS subqueries are rewritten into a semi-join with the outer query and then resolved via one of the semijoin strategies (firstmatch, loosescan, duplicateweedout, or materialization).

optimizer_switches.name: duplicateweedout
optimizer_switches.explanation: Semijoin strategy: runs the subquery as a normal inner join and then removes duplicate outer rows using a temporary table keyed on their row IDs.
node_labels: Remove duplicate t1 rows using temporary table (we...

suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans

executive_summary: Query scans 1 table; examines ~2,001 rows to return 49 in 0.35 ms. Main finding: no index covers (y, x) on t2.

query.raw: /* select#1 */ select `testdb`.`t1`.`id` AS `id`,`testdb`.`t1`.`a` AS `a`,`testdb`.`t1`.`b` AS `b`,`testdb`.`t1`.`c` AS `c`,`testdb`.`t1`.`d` AS `d` from `testdb`.`t1` semi join (`testdb`.`t2`) where ((`testdb`.`t1`.`id` = `testdb`.`t2`.`x`) and (`testdb`.`t2`.`y` < 100))
query.beautified:
select testdb.t1.id AS id,testdb.t1.a AS a,testdb.t1.b AS b,testdb.t1.c AS c,testdb.t1.d AS d
from testdb.t1 semi
  join (testdb.t2)
where ((testdb.t1.id = testdb.t2.x) and (testdb.t2.y < 100))

index_suggestions.ddl: CREATE INDEX idx_t2_y_x ON t2 (y, x);
index_suggestions.reason: Full scan on t2 with filter on (y, x)

operator_complexities.folded_label: Remove duplicate t1 rows using temporary table (weedout) starts=1 rows=49
operator_complexities.short_label: Remove duplicate t1 rows using temporary table (we...

complexity.rationale: DuplicateWeedout semijoin: runs as a plain inner join, then drops duplicates using a temp-table index on the outer row IDs.
Indexed nested loop: each outer row probes the inner table via an index descent.
Full table scan: the storage engine returns every row; cost scales with the table size.
Unique-key lookup (eq_ref): one B+tree descent into the inner table per outer row — as fast as MySQL can get.
